title: Measure Theory Attack: Probability Measures as the Δd=2 Engine

lebesgue.conjecture_check: YES — measure μ is the Δd=2 engine
lebesgue.note: Lebesgue: simple fn(0) → ∫dμ(2) = Δd=2; measure μ is explicit

lp_spaces.note: L^2.0 norm = EML-2; duality L^p*=L^q = EML-2; Hölder = EML-2

lebesgue.key_insight: Lebesgue ∫dμ: simple fn(EML-0) → integral(EML-2) = Δd=2; L^p norms all EML-2

radon_nikodym.conjecture_check: YES — μ is literally the measure being introduced
radon_nikodym.note: Radon-Nikodym: abstract ν(0) → dν/dμ density(2) = Δd=2

change_of_measure.note: Change of measure: P(EML-0) → log(dQ/dP)(EML-2) = Δd=2

radon_nikodym.key_insight: RN theorem: canonical Δd=2 — abstract measure(0) → density(2); KL=EML-2

simple_function.note: Σ a_i 1_{A_i} = EML-0 (finite sum of indicators)

radon_nikodym.note: dν/dμ = EML-2 density (Δd=2 from abstract measure)

fourier_transform_L2.note: F: L²→L² via oscillatory kernel = EML-3

depth_table.key_pattern: All EML-2 measure objects involve ∫ dμ or log(dμ): depth-2 = integration layer
depth_table.key_insight: Measure theory: EML-0 = abstract; EML-2 = integral/density; EML-3 = oscillatory

eml_depth_summary.EML-0: σ-algebras, abstract measures, simple functions, indicators
eml_depth_summary.EML-2: Lebesgue integral, L^p norms, Radon-Nikodym derivative, KL, cond. expectation
eml_depth_summary.EML-3: Fourier transform on L², oscillatory kernels
eml_depth_summary.EML-\u221e: Weak-* topology on measure spaces, non-separable cases

key_theorem: The EML Measure Theory Theorem (S212): In measure theory, the canonical depth jump is Δd=2: Abstract measure μ (EML-0) → Lebesgue integral ∫f dμ (EML-2). Abstract measure ν (EML-0) → Radon-Nikodym density dν/dμ (EML-2). Probability measure P (EML-0) → KL divergence D_KL (EML-2). The Radon-Nikodym theorem IS the Δd=2 theorem for measures: it precisely characterizes when an abstract measure gains a density (= gains EML depth 2). Measure theory confirms: 'adding a measure' → Δd=2 is a structural theorem.
rabbit_hole_log: Radon-Nikodym = canonical Δd=2: the density dν/dμ is exactly what appears at depth 2
All L^p norms = EML-2: integration layer universal across all p
Conditional expectation E[X|F] = EML-2: projection onto L²(F) = integration layer
